paper_title: Stable Water Isotopes and Sea Ice Influence on Climate

methods.method_name: Model Experimentation on Sea Ice Influence
methods.description: This method involves using climate models to study the influence of sea ice changes on the isotopic composition of precipitation over Greenland and Antarctica. The experiments aim to understand the impact of sea ice extent and sea surface temperature (SST) changes on isotopic responses.

steps.description: Create sea ice conditions using a coupled climate model forced by 2×, 4×, and 8× CO2 levels.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate sea ice conditions with varying CO2 levels using a climate model

steps.description: Use the generated sea ice and SST conditions to force atmospheric isotope models.
steps.searchable_summary: apply sea ice and SST conditions to atmospheric isotope models

steps.description: Analyze the variability in the isotope-temperature slope for the Greenland ice sheet.
